---
title: ""
pagetitle: "CV"
description: "Curriculum vitae of Dr. Erika C. Freeman, Junior Group Leader (Tenure Track) at IGB Berlin."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
  typst:
    papersize: a4
    margin: { x: 1.6cm, y: 1.5cm }
    fontsize: 9.5pt
---

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build.py from data/cv.yml + data/publications.yml. -->

:::: {.cv-page}

# Erika C. Freeman {.cv-name}

[Junior Group Leader (Tenure Track), IGB Berlin]{.cv-tagline}

[erika.freeman@igb-berlin.de · ORCID 0000-0001-7161-6038 · Google Scholar E6WubOUAAAAJ · erikacfreeman.com · Swiss & Canadian citizen · English (native), German (fluent, B2–C1), French (basic), Spanish (basic)]{.cv-meta}

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
[Download PDF](cv.pdf){.cv-download}
:::

## Current position

**Junior Group Leader (Tenure Track)** — group *Aquatic Biogeochemistry of Carbon* (ABC Lab), Leibniz-Institute of Freshwater Ecology and Inland Fisheries (IGB), Department 1 (Ecohydrology & Biogeochemistry); Programme Area 4. Since **January 2026**. Research programme: the *ecology of molecules* — dissolved organic matter (DOM) at molecular resolution (FT-ICR-MS) to predict and manage carbon-climate feedbacks and water quality.

## Appointments

| Role | Institution | Dates |
|---|---|---|
| Postdoctoral Researcher (with F. Altermatt) | Eawag (ETH Domain) & University of Zürich | 2023–2025 |
| Remote Team Facilitator, WHONDRS Consortium | PNNL (international, ~20 countries) | 2021–present |
| Program Coordinator, NSERC CREATE ABATE | (5-country) | 2016–2018 |
| Team Coordinator, Boreal 2050 | Cdn. Network for Aquatic Ecosystem Services | 2016–2018 |
| Research Assistant | Western University | 2013–2016 |

## Education

| Degree | Institution | Date | Supervisor(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| PhD, Plant Sciences | University of Cambridge | defended Apr 2023 | A. Tanentzap; E. Emilson (Canadian Forest Service) |
| MSc, Geography / Global Health Systems | Western University | 2018 | I. Creed |
| BSc, Environmental Science | Western University | 2016 |  |

## Research funding & analytical awards

- **DOE EMSL CAM #62138** (*Molecular Fingerprints of Metal Fate*), sole PI — **approved Feb 2026**; free PNNL FT-ICR-MS / NMR / multi-omics access.
- **DOE EMSL FICUS #62211** (*AI-Enabled Phytomining of Critical Minerals*), co-PI with N. Jafari (Texas A&M) — **invited to full proposal, Mar 2026**.
- **IGB start-up budget** €50,000.
- *Applications in preparation:* DFG Sachbeihilfe TRACE (Sept 2026); DFG Emmy Noether / FreshDreaMS; ERC Starting Grant WATERMARK (2027); Leibniz Junior Research Group VITALS (Mar 2028).

## Honours & awards

- **Gates Cambridge Scholarship** (2018–2023; ~1.3% acceptance rate).
- UTSC Postdoctoral Fellowship (2022–2024, declined).
- NSERC Canada Graduate Scholarship (M); Ontario Graduate Scholarship; NSERC USRA (2014, 2016); Cambridge fieldwork & travel awards.
- *Science* "Working Life" essay (2024), among the year's most-read; re-featured for International Women's Day 2025.

## Publications

Full record below. First author: **8 published**, 9 in review or in preparation. Co-author: **9 published**. Metrics via ORCID 0000-0001-7161-6038 and Google Scholar (E6WubOUAAAAJ).

**First-author**

- Freeman EC, Emilson EJS, Webster K, Dittmar T, Tanentzap AJ. Logging Disrupts the Ecology of Molecules in Headwater Streams. *PNAS* (2025).
- Freeman EC, McClure EA, Mudunuru ML, Ward CS, Bottos EM, González-Pinzón R, Feeser KL, Krause S, Peña J, Newcomer ME. Molecular Diversity of Dissolved Organic Matter Reflects Macroecological Patterns in River Networks. *Scientific Reports* (2025).
- Freeman EC, Peller T, Altermatt F. Ecosystem ecology needs an ecology of molecules. *Trends in Ecology and Evolution* (2025).
- Freeman EC, Padilla-Iglesias C. Racing the Clock. *Science* (2024).
- Freeman EC, Erratt KJ. Cyanobacteria in the Anthropocene: synanthropism forged in an era of global change. *Water Environmental Research* (2024).
- Freeman EC, Emilson EJS, Braga L, Emilson C, Martineau C, Dittmar T, Goldhammer T, Singer G, Tanentzap AJ. Universal Microbial Reworking of Dissolved Organic Matter along Environmental Gradients. *Nature Communications* (2024).
- Freeman EC, Jones B, Bergström AK, Creed IF. Global Changes May Be Promoting a Rise in Select Cyanobacteria in Nutrient-poor Northern Lakes. *Global Change Biology* (2020).
- Freeman EC, Erdozain M, Ouellet Dallaire C, Teichert S, Nelson HW, Creed IF. Demand for provisioning ecosystem services as a driver of change in the Canadian boreal zone. *Environmental Reviews* (2019).
- Freeman EC, Peller T, Keck F, Altermatt F. The Ecology of Molecules. *American Naturalist*, revised (R1); decision pending.
- Freeman EC. Rare Molecules as Community Architects. *Trends in Ecology and Evolution*, minor revision; acceptance pending.
- Freeman EC, Altermatt F. When molecules matter for ecosystem management. *Frontiers in Ecology and the Environment*, under review.
- Freeman EC. What precision medicine can teach biogeochemistry. *Nature Reviews Earth & Environment*, in prep.
- Freeman EC. The 0.52% problem: chemical surveillance and planetary health. *Lancet Planetary Health*, in prep.
- Freeman EC, Domisch S, Hilt S, McLeod A, Shatwell T, Schroer S, Tetzlaff D, Venohr M. Closing the freshwater verification gap. *Freshwater Ecology / L&O Letters*, re-homing after Nature Water.
- Freeman EC, Tanentzap AJ, Emilson EJS, Dittmar T. Detecting forest harvest through DOM fluorescence. *JGR: Biogeosciences*, in prep.
- Freeman EC, Altermatt F, Alter R. Leveraging community ecology to understand dissolved organic matter. *Frontiers in Ecology and the Environment*, in prep.
- Freeman EC. Reading the room at ASLO-SIL 2026: 1461 talks and the shape of a field. *L&O Bulletin*, submitted.

**Contributing-author**

- Matula E, Emilson EJS, Smenderovac E, Fonvielle J, **Freeman EC**, Tanentzap AJ. Wildfires Change Summertime Dissolved Organic Matter in Boreal Headwater Streams. *JGR Biogeosciences* (2025).
- Erratt KJ, Creed IF, **Freeman EC**, Trick C. Missing the Middle: Deep Cyanobacteria Layers Pose a "Known Unknown" Risk to Water Security. *Water Security* (2023).
- Stadler M, de Melo M, Garayburu-Caruso V, **Freeman EC**, Bice K, Mateus-Barros E, Lu Y, Barnard M, Cheng S, Dwivedi D, Tanentzap A, Meile C. Applying the Core-Satellite Species Concept: Characteristics of Rare and Common Riverine Dissolved Organic Matter. *Frontiers in Water* (2023).
- Buser-Young J, Garcia P, Schrenk M, Regier P, Ward ND, Biçe K, Brooks S, **Freeman EC**, Lønborg C. Determining the Biogeochemical Transformations of Organic Matter Composition in Global Rivers Using Molecular Signatures. *Frontiers in Water* (2023).
- Erratt KJ, Creed IF, **Freeman EC**, Westrick J, Trick CG, Birbeck J, Watson L, Zastepa A. Deep Cyanobacteria Layers: An Overlooked Aspect of Managing Risks of Cyanobacteria. *Environmental Science & Technology* (2022).
- Borton MA, Collins SM, Graham EB, Garayburu-Caruso VA, Goldman AE, de Melo M, **Freeman EC**, & WHONDRS Crowdsourced Consortium. It Takes a Village: Using a Crowdsourced Approach to Investigate Organic Matter Composition in Global Rivers Through the Lens of Ecological Theory. *Frontiers in Water* (2022).
- Deglint JL, Park JA, **Freeman EC**, Erratt KJ, Allaf MM, Wong A. Feasibility of Instance Segmentation of Phytoplankton Using Brightfield Microscopy & Deep Learning. *Journal of Computational Vision and Imaging Systems* (2021).
- Lembrechts J, Van den Hoogen J, Aalto J, Ashcroft M, De Frenne P, Kemppinen J, **Freeman EC**, & Hoffrén R. Global Maps of Soil Temperature. *Global Change Biology* (2021).
- Creed IF, Bergström AK, Trick CG, Grimm NB, Hessen DO, Karlsson J, Kidd KA, Kritzberg E, McKnight DM, **Freeman EC**, Senar OE, & others. Global Change-driven Effects on Dissolved Organic Matter Composition: Implications for Food Webs of Northern Lakes. *Global Change Biology* (2018).
- Beugnon, Cheneka et al. 2025. The Microclimate DataBase — turning the global SoilTemp project into an accessible archive for microclimate data. *Scientific Data*, in prep.
- Creed IF, Trick CG, **Freeman EC**, Oakes B. Protocol for the analysis of phytoplankton using the FlowCAM® from Fluid Imaging Technologies. Standard Operating Procedure 001-2016 (2016).

## Protocols

Publishing the group's DOM methods openly on protocols.io (sampling and preservation, FT-ICR-MS PPL-SPE workflow, AI-assisted molecular annotation); DOIs added as released.

## Invited talks (selected)

- *If more UHR DOM data is the answer, what was the question?* — ASLO-SIL 2026, Montreal.
- PLANET-DOM workshop, UFZ Leipzig (Sep 2025).
- Complex Organic Mixtures in Aquatic Systems (COMPASS), Delmenhorst (Mar 2025).
- BEEES seminar, University of Zürich (Mar 2025).
- *Synthesising DOM Across Ecosystems*, Skiathos (Oct 2023).

## Teaching & mentoring

- **Supervised** five Cambridge research projects / theses (G. Mezciems, B. Tan, J. Gayford, L. Mahony, W. Pallier) and two field technicians (K. Waxenberg, J. Schadenburg).
- **Teaching:** Teaching Assistant, Western University (2016–2018); guest lecture, FU Berlin freshwater-ecology seminar (Spring 2026).

## Service & esteem

- **Coordinator**, WHONDRS Consortium (PNNL; ~20 countries).
- **Reviewer:** *Science*, *Science Advances*, *Nature Communications*, *Limnology & Oceanography*, *Geochimica et Cosmochimica Acta*, *Communications Earth & Environment*, *Biogeosciences*.
- **Grant / fellowship review:** DFG (individual research grants); NSERC Discovery; GWIS National Fellowship.
- **Session chair:** ASLO 2027 (San Juan; with J. Wang); a DFG meeting session.
- Cross-IGB roles: co-leads the predictive-ecology working group; contributes to the Cross-cutting Theme/Platform (CTP) Data Management.

## Skills

Python, R, Google Earth Engine, bash, hydrological modeling (WhiteboxTools); statistics (GLM, mixed-effects, Bayesian, multivariate, machine learning); FT-ICR-MS / Orbitrap; AI annotation pipeline; geospatial analysis.

::::
